calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-13-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arnold Rugama for petitioner Enedina Rico; Jose Haro.

The Court observed that it had now received the declaration of the decedent's son Jose Haro, which supports the assertion that the property is community property and disclaims an interest in it. Based on that, and no opposition to the petition having been filed, the Court granted the petition, subject to review of the order. Mr. Rugama stated that the property was in foreclosure, and requested that the order be processed as quickly as possible to allow a new loan which would stop the foreclosure. The Court replied that the granting of the order would be reflected in today's minutes, and the signing of the order would proceed in due course.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Continued To Jun-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Continued To Jun-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Arthur Rugama for Petitioner.

The Court stated the problem is with conflicting allegations, that the petition states that community property was used for the down payment, then that the Son made the down payment, and that the Son paid the property taxes and is making the mortgage payment. Mr. Rugama stated that it was community property purchased during marriage as stated by the Petitioner, that he has been doing this for thirty years and no one has ever given him a straight story, that the supplement was verified, but the original wasn't, and the Decedent and the Spouse had savings in California. Mr. Rugama also stated that the Son suggested using the funds to buy the house, that he does not have credit, that the down payment came from the Father, that the Son has paid the mortgage as rent and that he has continued to make the payment. In addition, Mr. Rugama stated that the Bank is refusing payment, that the property is foreclosing, that there are seven Children and they have no interest, that it is too late to do a disclaimer and that the Wife wants the property. The Court suggests a basis on which the Son may have a claim and noted that the Son has not filed anything. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to June 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rugama needs to get what ever he plans to get done no later than May 31, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
